title: Goals Tab and Goal Alignment Loop
summary: Goals tab and proactive goal-alignment loop implemented and verified.

verification.notes: Implemented the Goals domain, web Goals tab, mobile API/client groundwork, assistant goal tools/context, open-loop goal context, and proactive model-backed Chief of Staff goal-alignment loop. Verified with mix compile; focused Goals tests; the mobile todo action-card privacy regression; and make build-mobile. Lint/typecheck were skipped under the repository's current production-first verification mode. Full Spectacula repo validation remains blocked by pre-existing unrelated manifest status errors elsewhere in docs/spectacula.

resume_context.last_completed_step: Moved manifest to done after required verification gates passed.
open_questions: Should native mobile replace Stream with Goals in the primary tab bar, or should Goals wait for a broader mobile navigation redesign?
Should person goals require explicit linked people, or can they begin as freeform goals and link people later?
Should sensitive goals influence priority silently when not shown, or should the assistant only use sensitive goals when it can mention the goal in the response?
Should goal review runs be visible in Stream, or only inside each goal detail?
Should goal progress state be user-editable, or only derived from progress updates and review results?
resume_context.notes: Plain Spectacula invocation, so final vetting is off. Open questions are being resolved using the assumptions already recorded in the spec unless Kent redirects.

history.note: Initial goals architecture draft created from repo context and existing Spectacula examples.
Narrow manifest sanity checks passed; global Spectacula validate is blocked by existing unrelated manifests.
User requested implementation and verification of the goals-tab-and-goal-alignment-loop spec.
mix compile, focused Goals tests, the mobile todo action-card privacy regression, and make build-mobile passed. Spec self-review passed; final vetting is off for the plain Spectacula invocation.
Implementation verified and ready for production deploy.
